def make_mock_signer(available=True):
    """Create a mock Signer trait object."""
    signer = MagicMock()
    if not available:
        signer.get_public_key.side_effect = Exception("signer unavailable")
    else:
        signer.get_public_key.return_value = b"\x00" * 32
        signer.sign_ed25519.return_value = b"\x00" * 64
        signer.sign_bbs.return_value = b"\x00" * 112
    return signer


def make_mock_storage(available=True):
    """Create a mock VaultStorage trait object."""
    storage = MagicMock()
    if not available:
        storage.store.side_effect = Exception("storage unavailable")
        storage.load.side_effect = Exception("storage unavailable")
    return storage


def make_mock_audit_log(available=True):
    """Create a mock AuditLog trait object."""
    audit = MagicMock()
    audit.recorded_events = []
    if not available:
        audit.record.side_effect = Exception("audit unavailable")
    else:
        def _record(event):
            audit.recorded_events.append(event)
        audit.record.side_effect = _record
    return audit

def make_committed_field(name, source_path, required=True):
    """Create a Committed SchemaField."""
    return SchemaField(
        name=name,
        kind=AttributeKind.Committed,
        source_path=ClaimPath(pointer=source_path),

def make_valid_schema(
    schema_id="test-schema-v1",
    version=1,
    fields=None,
    disclosure_rules=None,
    default_disclosure=DisclosureLevel.Never,
    description="Test schema",
):
    """Create a valid CredentialSchema."""
    if fields is None:
        fields = [
            make_raw_schema_field("name", "/personal/name"),
            make_derived_boolean_field(
                "age_over_21",
                "/personal/age",
                Predicate.GreaterThanOrEqual(21),
            ),
            make_committed_field("income", "/financial/income"),
        ]
    if disclosure_rules is None:
        disclosure_rules = [
            DisclosureRule(field_name=f.name, level=DisclosureLevel.Selectable)
            for f in fields
        ]
    return CredentialSchema(
        schema_id=schema_id,
        version=version,
        fields=fields,
        disclosure_policy=DisclosurePolicy(
            rules=disclosure_rules,
            default_level=default_disclosure,
        ),
        description=description,
    )

def make_engine_with_schema(schema=None, signer=None, storage=None, audit=None):
    """Create engine with a registered schema. Returns (engine, schema, signer, storage, audit)."""
    signer = signer or make_mock_signer()
    storage = storage or make_mock_storage()
    audit = audit or make_mock_audit_log()
    config = make_valid_config()
    engine = create_engine(config, signer, storage, audit)
    schema = schema or make_valid_schema()
    register_schema(engine, schema)
    return engine, schema, signer, storage, audit

class TestValidateSchema:
    """Tests for validate_schema — pure validation, no state mutation."""

    def test_validate_schema_happy_path(self):
        """Valid schema with all field kinds returns empty error list."""
        schema = make_valid_schema()
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=128)
        assert errors == [], f"Expected no errors for valid schema, got: {errors}"

    def test_validate_schema_duplicate_field_names(self):
        """Schema with duplicate field names returns error."""
        fields = [
            make_raw_schema_field("name", "/a"),
            make_raw_schema_field("name", "/b"),  # duplicate
        ]
        schema = make_valid_schema(fields=fields, disclosure_rules=[
            DisclosureRule(field_name="name", level=DisclosureLevel.Selectable),
        ])
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=128)
        assert len(errors) > 0, "Expected errors for duplicate field names"
        assert any("duplicate" in e.lower() or "unique" in e.lower() for e in errors), \
            f"Expected duplicate field error, got: {errors}"

    def test_validate_schema_derived_boolean_missing_predicate(self):
        """DerivedBoolean field without predicate returns error."""
        field = SchemaField(
            name="age_check",
            kind=AttributeKind.DerivedBoolean,
            source_path=ClaimPath(pointer="/age"),
            predicate=None,  # missing predicate
            required=True,
        )
        schema = make_valid_schema(fields=[field], disclosure_rules=[
            DisclosureRule(field_name="age_check", level=DisclosureLevel.Selectable),
        ])
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=128)
        assert len(errors) > 0, "Expected errors for missing predicate"
        assert any("predicate" in e.lower() for e in errors), \
            f"Expected predicate error, got: {errors}"

    def test_validate_schema_disclosure_policy_invalid_field_ref(self):
        """Disclosure policy referencing nonexistent field returns error."""
        fields = [make_raw_schema_field("name", "/name")]
        rules = [
            DisclosureRule(field_name="name", level=DisclosureLevel.Selectable),
            DisclosureRule(field_name="nonexistent", level=DisclosureLevel.Always),
        ]
        schema = make_valid_schema(fields=fields, disclosure_rules=rules)
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=128)
        assert len(errors) > 0, "Expected errors for invalid disclosure policy field reference"

    def test_validate_schema_attribute_limit_exceeded(self):
        """Schema exceeding max_attributes returns error."""
        fields = [make_raw_schema_field(f"field_{i}", f"/f{i}") for i in range(10)]
        rules = [DisclosureRule(field_name=f"field_{i}", level=DisclosureLevel.Selectable) for i in range(10)]
        schema = make_valid_schema(fields=fields, disclosure_rules=rules)
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=5)
        assert len(errors) > 0, "Expected errors when exceeding attribute limit"
        assert any("limit" in e.lower() or "exceed" in e.lower() or "max" in e.lower() for e in errors), \
            f"Expected attribute limit error, got: {errors}"

    def test_validate_schema_empty_fields(self):
        """Schema with empty fields list returns error."""
        schema = make_valid_schema(fields=[], disclosure_rules=[])
        errors = validate_schema(schema, max_attributes=128)
        assert len(errors) > 0, "Expected errors for empty fields list"


class TestEncodeClaimToScalar:
    """Tests for encode_claim_to_scalar — pure deterministic encoding."""

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_string_happy(self):
        """Encoding a StringVal with Utf8HashToScalar produces 32 bytes."""
        result = encode_claim_to_scalar(
            ClaimValue.StringVal("hello world"),
            ScalarEncoding.Utf8HashToScalar,
        )
        assert isinstance(result, bytes), "Expected bytes output"
        assert len(result) == 32, f"Expected 32 bytes, got {len(result)}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_int_happy(self):
        """Encoding an IntVal with Int64LittleEndian produces 32 bytes."""
        result = encode_claim_to_scalar(
            ClaimValue.IntVal(42),
            ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian,
        )
        assert isinstance(result, bytes), "Expected bytes output"
        assert len(result) == 32, f"Expected 32 bytes, got {len(result)}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_bool_happy(self):
        """Encoding a BoolVal with BoolSingleByte produces 32 bytes."""
        result = encode_claim_to_scalar(
            ClaimValue.BoolVal(True),
            ScalarEncoding.BoolSingleByte,
        )
        assert isinstance(result, bytes), "Expected bytes output"
        assert len(result) == 32, f"Expected 32 bytes, got {len(result)}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_bytes_happy(self):
        """Encoding a BytesVal with BytesDirect produces 32 bytes."""
        result = encode_claim_to_scalar(
            ClaimValue.BytesVal(b"\x01\x02\x03"),
            ScalarEncoding.BytesDirect,
        )
        assert isinstance(result, bytes), "Expected bytes output"
        assert len(result) == 32, f"Expected 32 bytes, got {len(result)}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_date_happy(self):
        """Encoding a DateVal with DateToEpochDays produces 32 bytes."""
        result = encode_claim_to_scalar(
            ClaimValue.DateVal("2024-01-15"),
            ScalarEncoding.DateToEpochDays,
        )
        assert isinstance(result, bytes), "Expected bytes output"
        assert len(result) == 32, f"Expected 32 bytes, got {len(result)}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_deterministic(self):
        """Same input always produces the same 32-byte scalar."""
        val = ClaimValue.StringVal("determinism test")
        enc = ScalarEncoding.Utf8HashToScalar
        r1 = encode_claim_to_scalar(val, enc)
        r2 = encode_claim_to_scalar(val, enc)
        r3 = encode_claim_to_scalar(val, enc)
        assert r1 == r2 == r3, "Encoding must be deterministic"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_encoding_mismatch(self):
        """Incompatible encoding scheme raises encoding_mismatch error."""
        with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc_info:
            encode_claim_to_scalar(
                ClaimValue.StringVal("hello"),
                ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian,  # mismatch: string with int encoding
            )
        err = exc_info.value
        assert _is_cred_error(err, CredError.EncodingFailed) or "mismatch" in str(err).lower(), \
            f"Expected encoding mismatch error, got: {err}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_value_out_of_range(self):
        """Integer too large for scalar raises value_out_of_range error."""
        # Use an astronomically large integer that exceeds BBS+ group order
        huge_int = 2**256
        with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc_info:
            encode_claim_to_scalar(
                ClaimValue.IntVal(huge_int),
                ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian,
            )
        err = exc_info.value
        assert _is_cred_error(err, CredError.EncodingFailed) or "range" in str(err).lower(), \
            f"Expected value out of range error, got: {err}"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_distinct_values_distinct_scalars(self):
        """Different ClaimValues map to different scalars (injectivity spot check)."""
        values = [
            (ClaimValue.IntVal(0), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian),
            (ClaimValue.IntVal(1), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian),
            (ClaimValue.IntVal(-1), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian),
            (ClaimValue.IntVal(2**31), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian),
        ]
        scalars = [encode_claim_to_scalar(v, e) for v, e in values]
        # All scalars should be unique
        assert len(set(scalars)) == len(scalars), \
            "Distinct ClaimValues should produce distinct scalars"

    def test_encode_claim_to_scalar_bool_distinct(self):
        """True and False produce distinct scalars."""
        true_scalar = encode_claim_to_scalar(ClaimValue.BoolVal(True), ScalarEncoding.BoolSingleByte)
        false_scalar = encode_claim_to_scalar(ClaimValue.BoolVal(False), ScalarEncoding.BoolSingleByte)
        assert true_scalar != false_scalar, "True and False must encode to distinct scalars"

            evaluate_predicate(Predicate.GreaterThan(18), ClaimValue.StringVal("hello"))
        err = exc_info.value
        assert _is_cred_error(err, CredError.InvalidPredicate) or "type" in str(err).lower() or "mismatch" in str(err).lower(), \
            f"Expected type mismatch error, got: {err}"

    def test_evaluate_predicate_in_set_empty(self):
        """InSet([]) raises empty_set error."""
        with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc_info:
            evaluate_predicate(Predicate.InSet([]), ClaimValue.StringVal("A"))
        err = exc_info.value
        assert _is_cred_error(err, CredError.InvalidPredicate) or "empty" in str(err).lower(), \
            f"Expected empty set error, got: {err}"

    def test_evaluate_predicate_negation_consistency(self):
        """GreaterThan and LessThanOrEqual produce complementary results."""
        test_values = [0, 1, 17, 18, 19, 100, -1, -100]
        threshold = 18
        for v in test_values:
            gt = evaluate_predicate(Predicate.GreaterThan(threshold), ClaimValue.IntVal(v))
            lte = evaluate_predicate(Predicate.LessThanOrEqual(threshold), ClaimValue.IntVal(v))
            assert gt != lte, (
                f"GreaterThan({threshold}) and LessThanOrEqual({threshold}) must be complements "
                f"for value {v}, got GT={gt}, LTE={lte}"
            )

class TestCheckExpiry:
    """Tests for check_expiry."""

    def test_check_expiry_not_expired(self):

        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()

        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
        result = check_expiry(engine, cred_id, now)

        assert isinstance(result, ExpiryCheckResult), "Should return ExpiryCheckResult"
        assert result.is_expired is False, "Credential should not be expired"
        assert result.remaining_seconds > 0, "Should have remaining seconds"
        assert result.status_updated is False, "No status transition should occur"

    def test_check_expiry_expired(self):
        """Check expiry past TTL transitions to Expired and records audit."""
        engine, schema, _, _, audit = make_engine_with_schema()
        claims = make_valid_claims()
        config = make_issuance_config(ttl=1, clock_skew=0)  # 1 second TTL, no tolerance
        bundle = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, config)
        cred_id = bundle.metadata.id

        # Check with a time far in the future
        future = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat()
        result = check_expiry(engine, cred_id, future)

        assert result.is_expired is True, "Credential should be expired"
        assert result.remaining_seconds == 0, "Remaining seconds should be 0"
        assert result.status_updated is True, "Status should have been updated"

    def test_check_expiry_clock_skew_tolerance(self):
        """Credential within clock skew tolerance is not yet expired."""
        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()
        claims = make_valid_claims()
        config = make_issuance_config(ttl=60, clock_skew=300)  # 1 min TTL, 5 min tolerance
        bundle = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, config)
        cred_id = bundle.metadata.id

        # Check at TTL+30s (within tolerance window of 300s)
        check_time = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=90)).isoformat()
        result = check_expiry(engine, cred_id, check_time)

        # With 300s tolerance, TTL of 60s means not expired until 60+300=360s after issuance
        assert result.is_expired is False, \
            "Credential within clock skew tolerance should not be expired"

    def test_check_expiry_exactly_at_boundary(self):
        """Credential checked at exactly expires_at + tolerance is expired."""
        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()
        claims = make_valid_claims()
        config = make_issuance_config(ttl=60, clock_skew=0)
        bundle = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, config)
        cred_id = bundle.metadata.id

        expires_at = datetime.fromisoformat(
            bundle.metadata.expires_at.replace("Z", "+00:00")
        )
        # Check at exactly expires_at (with 0 skew tolerance)
        result = check_expiry(engine, cred_id, expires_at.isoformat())
        assert result.is_expired is True, \
            "Credential should be expired at exactly expires_at with 0 tolerance"

    def test_check_expiry_one_second_before_boundary(self):
        """Credential checked 1 second before expiry is not expired."""

        claims = make_valid_claims()
        config = make_issuance_config(ttl=3600, clock_skew=0)

            bundle.metadata.expires_at.replace("Z", "+00:00")
        )
        one_before = (expires_at - timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat()
        result = check_expiry(engine, cred_id, one_before)
        assert result.is_expired is False, \
            "Credential should NOT be expired 1 second before boundary"

        """Non-existent credential raises credential_not_found."""

class TestInvariantEngineNeverHoldsKeyMaterial:

class TestInvariantErrorMessagesNoSecrets:
    """Invariant: Error messages NEVER contain key material, blinding factors, or raw claim values."""

    def test_invariant_error_messages_no_secrets(self):
        """No error detail message contains secret patterns."""
        # Generate various errors and check their messages
        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()

        errors_to_check = []

        # Schema not found
        try:
            issue_credential(engine, "nonexistent", make_valid_claims(), make_issuance_config())
        except Exception as e:
            errors_to_check.append(str(e))

        # Missing claim
        try:
            bad_claims = ClaimSet(
                claims={"/personal/age": ClaimValue.IntVal(30)},
                source_vault_id="v",
                retrieved_at="2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
            )
            issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, bad_claims, make_issuance_config())
        except Exception as e:
            errors_to_check.append(str(e))

        # Credential not found
        try:
            get_credential(engine, make_credential_id("0" * 32))
        except Exception as e:
            errors_to_check.append(str(e))

        # Check none of the error messages contain secret-like patterns
        secret_patterns = [
            r'[0-9a-f]{64}',  # 256-bit hex key
            r'blinding',  # blinding factor reference with value
            r'-----BEGIN',  # PEM key material
            r'secret_key',
            r'private_key',
        ]
        for msg in errors_to_check:
            msg_lower = msg.lower()
            # Error messages should not contain raw claim values
            assert "alice" not in msg_lower or "claim" not in msg_lower, \
                f"Error message may contain raw claim data: {msg}"
            assert "75000" not in msg, \
                f"Error message may contain raw numeric claim value: {msg}"
            for pattern in secret_patterns:
                # Allow credential IDs (32 hex chars) but not 64-char keys
                if pattern == r'[0-9a-f]{64}':
                    matches = re.findall(pattern, msg_lower)
                    for m in matches:
                        assert len(m) <= 32, \
                            f"Error message may contain key material: {msg}"

            bundle = issue_credential(engine, "default-never-test", claims, make_issuance_config())

            # Try to disclose implicit_field — should fail
            with pytest.raises(Exception):
                record_presentation(
                    engine, bundle.metadata.id, Domain(value="v.com"),
                    ["explicit_field", "implicit_field"]  # implicit_field should be Never
                )
        except Exception:
            # Schema validation rejected it — also valid (fail-closed)
            pass


class TestInvariantAuditOnEveryStateChange:
    """Invariant: Every state-changing operation emits an audit event."""

    def test_invariant_audit_on_every_state_change(self):
        """All state-changing operations emit audit events."""
        signer = make_mock_signer()
        storage = make_mock_storage()
        audit = make_mock_audit_log()
        config = make_valid_config()
        engine = create_engine(config, signer, storage, audit)

        # Track audit call count
        initial_count = audit.record.call_count

        # 1. Register schema (state change)
        schema = make_valid_schema()
        register_schema(engine, schema)
        after_register = audit.record.call_count
        assert after_register > initial_count, \
            "Schema registration must emit audit event"

        # 2. Issue credential (state change)
        claims = make_valid_claims()
        bundle = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, make_issuance_config())
        after_issue = audit.record.call_count
        assert after_issue > after_register, \
            "Credential issuance must emit audit event"

        # 3. Record presentation (state change)
        disclosed = [f.name for f in schema.fields]
        record_presentation(engine, bundle.metadata.id, Domain(value="v.com"), disclosed)
        after_present = audit.record.call_count
        assert after_present > after_issue, \
            "Credential presentation must emit audit event"

        # 4. Issue another credential for revocation test
        bundle2 = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, make_issuance_config())
        before_revoke = audit.record.call_count

        # 5. Revoke credential (state change)
        revoke_credential(engine, bundle2.metadata.id, "test")
        after_revoke = audit.record.call_count
        assert after_revoke > before_revoke, \
            "Credential revocation must emit audit event"


class TestInvariantScalarEncodingDeterministic:

        """Same input always produces same output across multiple calls."""
        test_cases = [
            (ClaimValue.StringVal("hello"), ScalarEncoding.Utf8HashToScalar),
            (ClaimValue.IntVal(42), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian),
            (ClaimValue.BoolVal(True), ScalarEncoding.BoolSingleByte),
            (ClaimValue.BoolVal(False), ScalarEncoding.BoolSingleByte),
            (ClaimValue.DateVal("2024-01-01"), ScalarEncoding.DateToEpochDays),
        ]

        for value, encoding in test_cases:
            results = [encode_claim_to_scalar(value, encoding) for _ in range(10)]
            assert all(r == results[0] for r in results), \
                f"Encoding for {value} with {encoding} must be deterministic"

class TestInvariantScalarEncodingInjectivity:
    """Invariant: Distinct ClaimValues should map to distinct scalars (spot check)."""

    def test_invariant_scalar_injectivity_integers(self):
        """Random integer pairs produce distinct scalars."""
        random.seed(42)
        seen = set()
        for _ in range(100):
            v = random.randint(-2**31, 2**31)
            scalar = encode_claim_to_scalar(
                ClaimValue.IntVal(v), ScalarEncoding.Int64LittleEndian
            )
            assert scalar not in seen or v in seen, \
                f"Collision detected for IntVal({v})"
            seen.add(scalar)

    def test_invariant_scalar_injectivity_strings(self):
        """Random string pairs produce distinct scalars."""
        random.seed(42)
        strings = [f"test_string_{i}" for i in range(100)]
        scalars = [
            encode_claim_to_scalar(ClaimValue.StringVal(s), ScalarEncoding.Utf8HashToScalar)
            for s in strings
        ]
        assert len(set(scalars)) == len(scalars), \
            "Distinct strings should produce distinct scalars"


class TestInvariantPedersenCommitmentHiding:
    """Invariant: Same value with different blinding factors produces different commitments."""

    def test_invariant_pedersen_commitment_hiding(self):
        """Issuing same committed value twice produces different commitment bytes."""
        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()
        claims = make_valid_claims()

        bundle1 = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, make_issuance_config())
        bundle2 = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, make_issuance_config())

        committed1 = [a for a in bundle1.bbs.attributes if a.kind == AttributeKind.Committed]
        committed2 = [a for a in bundle2.bbs.attributes if a.kind == AttributeKind.Committed]

        assert len(committed1) > 0, "Should have committed attributes"
        assert len(committed1) == len(committed2)

        for c1, c2 in zip(committed1, committed2):
            # Same value, different blinding factors → different commitments (with overwhelming probability)
            assert c1.committed.commitment.commitment_bytes != c2.committed.commitment.commitment_bytes, \
                f"Pedersen commitments for '{c1.committed.name}' should differ due to fresh blinding factors"


class TestInvariantCredentialExpiredPresentation:
    """Invariant: Expired credentials cannot be presented."""

    def test_invariant_expired_credential_cannot_be_presented(self):
        """Expired credential presentation is denied."""
        engine, schema, _, _, _ = make_engine_with_schema()
        claims = make_valid_claims()
        config = make_issuance_config(ttl=1, clock_skew=0)
        bundle = issue_credential(engine, schema.schema_id, claims, config)
        cred_id = bundle.metadata.id

        # Force expiry
        import time
        time.sleep(2)

        # Attempt to present — should be denied due to TTL check
        disclosed = [f.name for f in schema.fields]
        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            record_presentation(engine, cred_id, Domain(value="v.com"), disclosed)

def _is_cred_error(exc, expected_kind):
    """Check if an exception is a CredErrorDetail with the expected kind."""
    if isinstance(exc, CredErrorDetail):
        return exc.kind == expected_kind
    if hasattr(exc, 'kind'):
        return exc.kind == expected_kind
    # Check string representation as fallback
    return expected_kind.name.lower() in str(exc).lower() if hasattr(expected_kind, 'name') else False
